fixes.description: Add pre-dispatch validation in the primary FSM. Before invoking any writer sub-module, check if ctx['flurslex'] is approaching the threshold. If it would exceed the limit, transition to a safe error-handling state instead of blindly dispatching.
fixes.fixed_code:
# CausalTrace Fix: Pre-dispatch boundary guard
        _CTX_THRESHOLD = 16
        if self._state["ctx"].get("flurslex", 0) >= _CTX_THRESHOLD:
            _ret = 2  # Signal overflow error
            _next_cycle = self._state["cycle"] + 1
        else:
            _ret, _next_cycle = {submod}.invoke(
                hint=_hint, span=_span, caller={STATE},
                ctx=self._state["ctx"], cycle=self._state["cycle"])

fixes.description: Pass a copy of ctx to each sub-module and validate the result before committing. This prevents any single sub-module from silently corrupting the shared state beyond safe bounds.
Modify writer modules to check the current value before incrementing. If flurslex is already at or above threshold-1, skip the increment.
fixes.fixed_code:
_old = self._state["ctx"].get(_CTX_WRITES_KEY, 0)
        _inc = 2 if hint == 2 else 1
        # CausalTrace Fix: Self-limiting write guard
        if _old + _inc < 16:
            self._state["ctx"][_CTX_WRITES_KEY] = _old + _inc
